title: Observability Setup
text: Observability Setup To use observability feature in Uniface, you need to configure the required settings as explained in the following sections. Configuration Overview By default, Observability is disabled. To enable it, add the following section to your application's ASN file: Explain this code [OBSERVABILITY] TRACE = on LOG = on Copy Uniface uses the OTLP HTTP Exporter to send telemetry data to any backend that supports OTLP. Configure endpoints in your ASN file as follows: Explain this code TRACE_ENDPOINT = http://yourhost:port/v1/traces LOG_ENDPOINT = http://yourhost:port/v1/logs Copy Note: TLS is not supported. Additional data to trace spans The following setting to trace spans are disabled by default. You can switch them on to add additional telemetry data to trace spans. Explain this code ; Add $status as span attribute ADD_PROC_STATUS = off ; Add $proc_error as span attribute ADD_PROC_ERROR = off ; Add ProcScript profiling as span attributes ADD_PROCPROFILING = off ; Add ProcScript tracing as span events ADD_PROCTRACING = off Copy Span Processing Configuration These options control how trace spans are buffered and exported. All collected spans are first added to an internal queue, waiting to be processed. Once the queue reaches its maximum capacity, any new spans will be dropped until space becomes available again. Spans in the queue are processed in batches, based on one of the following conditions: The number of queued spans reaches the defined batch size ( BATCH_SIZE ) The defined time interval ( SCHEDULE_DELAY ) has elapsed. A configuration example: Explain this code ; Max number of spans in the buffer QUEUE_SIZE = 2048 ; Max spans exported in a batch BATCH_SIZE = 512 ; Time (ms) between exports SCHEDULE_DELAY = 5000 ; Max time (ms) to flush spans on shutdown FLUSH_TIMEOUT = 5000 Copy These values should be adjusted based on your system's environment—such as available memory, network bandwidth, and the volume of telemetry data generated by your application. Recommendation: Higher throughput applications should increase QUEUE_SIZE and BATCH_SIZE to reduce data loss and optimize performance. Latency-sensitive applications might prefer shorter SCHEDULE_DELAY for faster export. Always balance performance with resource usage based on your deployment scenario. Configuration Reference [OBSERVABILITY] Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
